name: Focaccia
summary: A dimpled, olive-oil-rich flatbread that's hard to get wrong.

ingredients.item: Fresh or dried herbs (e.g. rosemary, thyme, oregano) — optional
Optional toppings: sliced olives, tomatoes, garlic, red onion

tips: Cold fermentation is optional but recommended for better flavor and texture. From step 3, cover edges and refrigerate.
The dough will be sticky — that's normal. Use wet or oiled hands when handling it.
Use sea or flaky salt and spelt for a rustic touch, or whole wheat for extra flavor.
Focaccia freezes well. Let it cool completely before freezing in slices.

steps.title: Mix the Dough
steps.instruction: Mix the sourdough starter, water, sugar, and olive oil until mostly combined. Add the flour and salt, then mix until a shaggy, sticky dough forms. No need to knead at this stage.

steps.title: Rest & Stretch and Folds
steps.instruction: Cover the dough and let it rest 30 minutes. Then perform 2–3 sets of stretch and folds, one set every 30–45 minutes, to strengthen the gluten. The timer covers the initial rest and each fold interval — reset it after every fold.
steps.tip: The dough is wet and sticky — wet your hands so it doesn't stick during folds.

steps.title: Bulk Fermentation
steps.instruction: After the final fold, cover the bowl lightly and let it ferment at room temperature until noticeably puffy and expanded — about 6–8 hours.

steps.title: Cold Fermentation (Recommended)
steps.instruction: Transfer the dough to an oiled container and refrigerate overnight (8–24 hours). This step enhances flavor and makes the dough easier to handle.
steps.tip: Optional but recommended for the best flavor and texture.

steps.title: Shape & Second Rise
steps.instruction: Grease a baking pan generously with olive oil. Transfer the dough to the pan and gently stretch it to fill the edges. If it resists, let it rest for 10–15 minutes, then try again. Cover and let it rise at room temperature for 1–2 hours until bubbly.

steps.title: Dimple & Add Toppings
steps.instruction: Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C). Drizzle the dough with more olive oil. Use oiled fingers to press deep dimples across the surface. Add herbs, coarse sea salt, and optional toppings of your choice.

steps.title: Bake
steps.instruction: Bake for 20–25 minutes, or until golden brown and crisp on the edges. Rotate the pan halfway through for even browning.

steps.title: Cool & Serve
steps.instruction: Remove from the oven and let it cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack. Slice and enjoy warm or at room temperature.
